meta.line1: Agreement governing the use of the TyPlace platform.
meta.line2: The current version is published on this page.
meta.line3: For questions about documents, requests, and disputes, please use the Contacts page and the Help Center.

note.text1: TyPlace is a platform where users enter into transactions with each other. The platform does not sell digital goods or services in its own name, but provides listings, order creation, payment for a specific transaction, a safe transaction mechanism, chat, support, and dispute resolution tools.

general.title: 1. General Provisions
general.p1: 1.1. This User Agreement governs the use of the TyPlace platform and its functionality, including posting listings, creating orders, paying for specific transactions, using the safe transaction mechanism, support, and other related functions.
general.p2: 1.2. By using the platform, the user confirms that they have read the terms of this Agreement and accept them in full.
general.p3: 1.3. Other platform documents also apply together with this Agreement, including the Platform Rules, Privacy Policy, and Cookie Policy.

defs.title: 2. Terms and Definitions
defs.platformText: the TyPlace website and related interfaces and functions.
defs.sellerText: a user who posts a listing and offers a digital good or service.
defs.buyerText: a user who purchases a digital good or service through the platform.
defs.listingText: a user-posted offer to sell a digital good or provide a digital service.
defs.orderText: a specific transaction created on the platform after the buyer places and pays for the relevant offer.
defs.balanceText: the amount of money paid by the buyer for a specific order through the payment functionality available on the platform.
defs.escrowText: a mechanism under which the payment amount for a specific order is held within that transaction until performance is confirmed, a refund is made, or a dispute decision is reached.
defs.supportText: the platform support service that reviews user requests.
defs.disputeText: platform staff members authorized to review disputes and make decisions within the platform’s functionality.

acceptance.title: 3. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.p1: 3.1. Registration, account login, posting listings, creating orders, paying for a specific transaction, and any other use of the platform’s functions constitutes full and unconditional acceptance of this Agreement.
acceptance.p2: 3.2. If the user does not agree with the terms of this Agreement, they must stop using the platform.

capacity.title: 4. Legal Capacity and Use of the Platform
capacity.p1: 4.1. The user is solely responsible for ensuring that they have the right to use the platform and enter into transactions in accordance with the laws of their country and other applicable requirements.
capacity.p2: 4.2. If the use of the platform, making purchases, sales, paying for transactions, or receiving payouts requires parental consent, consent of legal representatives, or compliance with other conditions, the user must ensure compliance with such requirements on their own.

account.title: 5. Account
account.p1: 5.1. Registration of an account is required to access the extended functionality of the platform.
account.p2: 5.2. The user must keep access to their account, devices, email, confirmation codes, and other authentication means secure.
account.p3: 5.3. It is prohibited to transfer an account to third parties, use someone else’s account, or create accounts to bypass restrictions, blocks, platform rules, or other security measures.
account.p4: 5.4. The platform may restrict access to an account, request proof of account ownership, or apply other protective measures if there is suspicion of compromise, abuse, or rule violations.

status.title: 6. Platform Status
status.p1: 6.1. TyPlace is a technological and intermediary platform that provides users with infrastructure for posting listings, communication, placing orders, paying for a specific transaction, using a safe transaction, support, and dispute resolution.
status.p2: 6.2. The platform is not the ordinary seller of digital goods and services posted by users and does not become a party to the transaction between the seller and the buyer unless explicitly stated otherwise on the platform.
status.p3: 6.3. The platform may set rules for the use of the service, restrictions, limits, fees, security requirements, and other operating conditions.

listings.title: 7. Listings
listings.p1: 7.1. The seller posts listings independently and is responsible for their content, accuracy, legality, completeness, and consistency with the subject of the offer.
listings.p2: 7.2. The seller must specify the essential terms of the offer, including the description of the digital good or service, important restrictions, transfer format, performance conditions, and other significant information.
listings.p3: 7.3. By posting a listing, the seller confirms that they have the right to offer the relevant good or service and that the posting of the listing does not violate third-party rights, platform rules, or other applicable restrictions.
listings.p4: 7.4. The platform may limit the visibility of a listing, hide, deactivate, remove, or otherwise restrict a listing in case of rule violations, suspicion of fraud, inaccurate information, or other grounds related to platform security.

prohibited.title: 8. Prohibited Goods, Services, and Actions
prohibited.p1: 8.1. The following may not be posted, transferred, bought, sold, or otherwise used on the platform:
prohibited.l1: illegal digital goods, services, and content, as well as items obtained unlawfully;
prohibited.l2: stolen accounts, digital assets, access credentials, items, and other objects whose origin is connected to hacking, fraud, or unauthorized access;
prohibited.l3: phishing, carding, malicious software, exploit services, hacking services, protection bypass services, or services aimed at obtaining unauthorized access;
prohibited.l4: personal data, databases, documents, payment data, details, and other confidential information if their posting, transfer, or use violates third-party rights or security requirements;
prohibited.l5: fraudulent schemes, deceptive services, spam services, manipulation, artificial boosting, and other unfair offers;
prohibited.l6: content that infringes intellectual property rights, brand rights, account rights, digital content rights, or other third-party rights;
prohibited.l7: other goods, services, and actions that the platform considers dangerous, unacceptable, prohibited, or incompatible with the safe operation of the service.
prohibited.p2: 8.2. The platform may apply measures including restricting functions, removing content, blocking an account, restricting operations, and taking other reasonable measures to protect the service and its users.

balance.title: 9. Transaction Payment and Holding of Funds
balance.p1: 9.1. On the platform, the buyer pays only for a specific order. Advance top-up of a general user balance for future use is not available.
balance.p2: 9.2. After the order is paid, the relevant amount is assigned to that transaction and held within the safe transaction mechanism until performance is confirmed, a refund is made, or a dispute decision is reached.
balance.p3: 9.3. The platform is not described or used as an electronic wallet, a system for freely storing user funds, or a service for unlimited storage of money outside a specific transaction.
balance.p4: 9.4. After the transaction is completed, the seller becomes eligible for payout in the manner provided by the platform rules, the platform interface, and the applicable transaction processing logic.

orders.title: 10. Order and Safe Transaction
orders.p1: 10.1. An order is created after the buyer places and pays for the relevant listing through the platform functionality.
orders.p2: 10.2. After the order is created, the payment amount is held within the safe transaction mechanism until performance is confirmed, a refund is made, or a dispute decision is reached.
orders.p3: 10.3. The platform records the essential transaction data at the time the order is created to the extent necessary to confirm the contents of the transaction and to review future disputes.
orders.p4: 10.4. Changes to a listing after an order is created do not automatically change the terms of the already created transaction.

execution.title: 11. Order Performance
execution.p1: 11.1. The seller must perform the order in accordance with the listing, the parties’ arrangements, and the nature of the digital good or service.
execution.p2: 11.2. The buyer must interact with the seller in good faith, provide the necessary information for performance if required, and confirm performance after the seller has actually fulfilled their obligations.
execution.p3: 11.3. The parties must, where possible, use the chat and platform tools to record terms, materials, and significant circumstances of order performance.

completion.title: 12. Order Confirmation, Refund, and Transaction Completion
completion.p1: 12.1. While the order is in active status, the buyer may confirm completion of the order, and the seller may issue a refund through the available platform functionality if allowed by the order logic.
completion.p2: 12.2. After the order is confirmed, the standard transaction cycle is considered completed, and the seller becomes eligible for payout in the manner provided by the platform rules.
completion.p3: 12.3. After the order is completed, the user may contact support regarding an issue with the order or file an appeal through the available platform tools; however, such contact does not in itself mean an automatic refund, cancellation of the transaction, or review of the outcome.
completion.p4: 12.4. A refund, review, or other measures regarding a completed transaction are possible only within the request review procedure, the platform rules, and when grounds recognized by the platform as sufficient exist.

disputes.title: 13. Disputes, Requests, and Appeals
disputes.p1: 13.1. In the event of a dispute regarding an active order, the user may contact platform support through the available request forms.
disputes.p2: 13.2. Disputes and requests are reviewed by support and/or authorized platform dispute resolution staff; in some cases, a decision may be made by the platform administrator within their authority.
disputes.p3: 13.3. Users must cooperate with the platform in good faith during the review of a dispute or request, provide relevant information, and not abuse the request system.
disputes.p4: 13.4. When reviewing a dispute or request, the platform may take into account the content of the listing, order data, platform correspondence, materials, attachments, the history of the parties’ actions, and other information available to the platform.

fees.title: 14. Platform Fee and Seller Payout
fees.p1: 14.1. The platform may charge a fee and other service charges expressly provided for in the interface for the use of certain functions and transaction support. The platform’s revenue is generated from a fee charged on a successful transaction unless otherwise expressly stated in the platform interface.
fees.p2: 14.2. After the transaction is completed, the seller becomes eligible for payout in the manner provided by the platform rules. Such payout may require internal processing, verification, confirmation, or may be temporarily restricted in the event of a dispute, signs of a violation, security requirements, or other material circumstances.
fees.p3: 14.3. The user must provide correct payment details and other information necessary to receive a payout and is responsible for errors in such information.

moderation.title: 15. Moderation, Restrictions, and Security Measures
moderation.p1: 15.1. To protect users and the platform, TyPlace may:
moderation.l1: restrict access to an account or отдельных platform functions;
moderation.l2: hide, deactivate, or remove a listing, message, attachment, or other content;
moderation.l3: restrict the placement of new orders, the support of certain transactions, the availability of seller payout, or the use of other platform functions;
moderation.l4: temporarily suspend transaction completion, a refund, payout availability, or another action related to an order where there is risk, a dispute, or signs of a violation;
moderation.l5: apply other reasonable measures necessary for the security of the platform, users, and internal verification procedures.
moderation.p2: 15.2. The platform determines the scope and nature of such measures at its discretion, taking into account the circumstances, available information, the nature of the violation, and the risks to the service.

checks.title: 16. Checks, Anti-Fraud, and the Right to Request Information
checks.p1: 16.1. The platform may conduct internal checks of accounts, operations, orders, requests, transaction-related submissions, and other user actions.
checks.p2: 16.2. Where there are signs of fraud, abuse, violation of platform rules, security requirements, or restrictions imposed by partners, the platform may request additional information, explanations, or supporting materials.
checks.p3: 16.3. If such information is not provided, or if the risk remains, the platform may restrict user functions, transaction operations, seller payout availability, access to the account, or apply other measures provided for by the platform’s internal logic.

ip.title: 17. Intellectual Property
ip.p1: 17.1. Rights to the interface, design, code, TyPlace brand, and other platform elements belong to the platform and/or the respective rights holders.
ip.p2: 17.2. The user is responsible for the legality of the content, descriptions, images, materials, and other objects they post or use on the platform.
ip.p3: 17.3. It is prohibited to copy, extract, distribute, or use platform elements and user materials outside permitted cases without appropriate grounds.

privacy.title: 18. Personal Data and Related Documents
privacy.p1: 18.1. The processing of personal data is carried out in accordance with the
privacy.p2: 18.2. The use of cookies and similar technologies is governed by the

liability.title: 19. Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: 19.1. The platform is provided on an “as is” basis, within the limits of actually available functionality, and without any guarantee of uninterrupted, error-free, or continuous operation.
liability.p2: 19.2. The platform does not guarantee that users will fulfill their obligations to each other, that abuse will not occur, that every transaction will be completed successfully, or that the expected result will be achieved for any particular digital good or service.
liability.p3: 19.3. The platform is not liable for user actions outside the limits of its actual role as a technological and intermediary platform, except where otherwise directly required by mandatory applicable law.

changes.title: 20. Changes to the Terms
changes.p1: 20.1. The platform may change this Agreement by publishing the current version on this page or by another method within the platform.
changes.p2: 20.2. Continued use of the platform after the publication of changes means that the user accepts the updated version of the Agreement.

final.title: 21. Final Provisions
final.p1: 21.1. If any provision of this Agreement is found to be unenforceable or invalid, the remaining provisions remain in force.
final.p2: 21.2. When using the platform, the user must also comply with the Platform Rules and other published platform documents.
final.p3: 21.3. All questions regarding the use of the platform, requests, disputes, and documents must be sent through the available platform communication channels.
